highlights: Golden sand beach beneath iconic Mũi Đại Lãnh Lighthouse with striking sunrise views
One of Vietnam's earliest sunrise locations with panoramic coastal vistas
Secluded, untouched beach with dramatic rock formations and minimal crowds

thingsToKnow.crowds: Significantly less crowded than Nha Trang or Da Nang beaches
thingsToKnow.difficulty: Several hundred meters of sand walking required to reach beach; steep lighthouse climb in hot conditions
thingsToKnow.safety: Swimming not allowed; dangerous rip currents year-round; local residents warn against swimming
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Limited facilities and vendors; no shade on main beach; tree shade available at far left corner
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Rainy season reduces swimming opportunities; strong winds common

visitorTips: Arrive early morning for sunrise photography; bring umbrella, sunscreen, and plenty of water
Find shade and picnic spot in far left beach corner beneath trees
Consider viewing lighthouse from nearby cliff instead of paying entry if budget-conscious

faq.question: Can I swim at Bãi Môn?
faq.answer: No, swimming is not allowed due to dangerous rip currents year-round. Local residents also advise against swimming.

faq.question: What are the entrance fees?
faq.answer: Beach entrance costs 20,000-30,000 VND per person; parking 5,000-20,000 VND depending on vehicle type.

faq.question: What should I bring?
faq.answer: Bring umbrella, sunscreen, plenty of water, and snacks as few vendors available and no shade on main beach.

sentiment.positive: Most visitors praise pristine scenery, golden sand, clear waters, lighthouse views, and peaceful atmosphere ideal for photography
sentiment.negative: Repeated complaints about garbage on beach, no swimming allowed despite paid entry, high fees, overcrowded parking, and poor lighthouse access
